_doc: Ground-truth gesture windows per sample video. Each entry: gesture_windows is a list of [start_sec, end_sec] ranges where a complete shisa-kanko gesture is performed. Empty list = no gesture should fire (true negative). 'skip: true' excludes a video from the harness (e.g. cartoon characters that DWPose cannot detect). Annotations seeded from the 2026-04-29 manual audit; refine as you watch each clip more carefully.

05_SHI_point_and_call.mp4.notes: Original POKETENASHI SHI section split. Real factory worker raises right arm in pointing pose.

SHI_point_and_call_spkepcmwi.mp4.notes: SPKEPCMWI factory video SHI section. Actor's full demo. Currently FN — far-field actor + low keypoint scores.

shisa_kanko_correct_demo.mp4.skip_reason: Animated mascot ヨシだ君, not a photographic human. DWPose cannot detect cartoons.

shisa_kanko_railway_toyota.mp4.notes: Real railway / Toyota worker pointing-and-calling sequences. v1.5 frame inspection showed the early window (t≈20, t≈113) is also valid shisa-kanko — the train DRIVER pointing at cab controls/signals from inside the cab, which is exactly the same Japanese safety standard the platform worker performs in the later window. Originally these were marked as FPs but they are genuine TPs of a different actor in the same video. Two windows now cover both: cab driver [10, 140] and platform worker [148, 215].

shisa_kanko_promotion_method.mp4.notes: Japanese promotional video showing continuous shisa-kanko demonstrations.

POKETENASHI.mp4.notes: Full POKETENASHI source. SHI section 174-217 s. Approach polygon set to the right-curb area where the SHI-section actor stands (verified at t=205, box bottom-center ≈0.6,0.85). Tightened to x≥0.6 on 2026-04-29 to exclude TE-stairs actor at t=132 (box bottom-center ≈0.5,0.85). Top edge y=0.7 excludes high-on-stairs scenes.

POKETENASHI_spkepcmwi_full.mp4.notes: Full SPKEPCMWI source. SHI demonstration t=158-175 — debug CSV showed the SHI actor's hip is at normalized x≈0.93 (FAR RIGHT, not left as initially assumed; the earlier t=162 sample frame showed forklift+worker but the foreground worker performing the gesture was on the right out of the visible-thumbnail frame). NA-section forklift driver at t=117 has hip at x≈0.43 (center). Recap-card portraits at t=204+ are at x≈0.55. Right-side polygon catches the SHI actor and excludes both the NA forklift and the recap cards.

POKETENASHI_autotech_indonesia_senam.mp4.notes: Group calisthenics / SENAM demo. Refined 2026-04-29 from frame inspection: t=90 s shows all 5 workers performing shisa-kanko (caption 'Tunjuk kanan dan kiri saat melewati persimpangan jalan'); t=95 transitions to outro 'CHALLENGE! SENAM POKETENASHI Untuk Semua Karyawan'. The earlier t=37.6 s events fall in the KE section (group salute) and are real FPs — group calisthenics in non-SHI sections still trigger the rule because there's no FSM polygon for this video.

POKETENASHI_anzen_daiichi_lecture.mp4.notes: Presenter-only lecture. NO gesture windows. Per-track FSM with `approach_zone_norm` set to a tiny far-left strip — the lecturer's foot point is on the right side of the frame so he never enters this polygon, and the rule never arms. With zero approach polygon configured, the orchestrator falls back to 'always armed' (current behavior); we set a non-empty polygon HERE so the FSM gate actually fires and clears the 51 lecture FPs.
